verdict: Endpoint closes for a competitor iff K_floor<26.2. ALL enumerated competitors have K_floor<=12<<26.2 -> endpoint closes RIGOROUSLY (strong-evidence) over the enumerated/lattice class. The thin joint was a worst-case DENSE artefact. Residual: worst-case K_floor<26.2 over the dense admissible class = DR-2/circle-incidence (B1's unrestricted-class frontier). The endpoint adds NO new obstruction. B1 T6 on {H-LAYER}.

claims.name: threshold_from_corrected_tail
claims.detail: (corrected tail 0.059 closes iff joint>1/(1-0.059)=1.0627 <=> rho>9.82 <=> K_floor < K_budget/rho-1 = 26.2)

claims.name: enumerated_competitors_close_rigorously
claims.detail: (every enumerated crystallographic competitor has K_floor <= 12.0 << threshold 26.2 (factor >= 2.2); the endpoint closes for the enumerated/lattice class with a LARGE margin, far from thin -- the thin joint 1.040-1.082 was the worst-case DENSE artefact, not the actual competitors)

claims.name: dense_worstcase_exceeds_threshold
claims.detail: (the proved bound K_floor<=T'(1-1/n) allows K_floor up to ~40 for a dense n~40 pattern with T'~n_pack -- EXCEEDS the threshold 26.2. So the endpoint is OPEN for hypothetical dense competitors with K_floor>26.2; this is the additive-energy/circle-incidence residual = DR-2)

claims.name: res5_endpoint_equals_b1_dr2_structure
claims.detail: (K_floor: enumerated <= 12.0 < threshold 26.2 < dense worst-case <= 40. The RES-5 endpoint closes RIGOROUSLY for the enumerated/lattice class (B1's T6 scope) and is DR-2-gated for the general dense class -- EXACTLY B1's existing structure. The endpoint adds NO new obstruction beyond DR-2)
